tool.name: Phase E graph edges
tool.description: extract gene-pair edges from PGGB graph path co-membership
tool.command: mkdir -p stage && ## The bundled script globs BED files by filename and uses each file's stem as ## the strain name. Stage every collection element as <element_identifier>.bed ## so the stem == strain. Mirror the pansn_rename / pair_strains pattern. #for $el in $annotations ln -s '$el' 'stage/${el.element_identifier}.bed' && #end for python '$__tool_directory__/phase_e_graph_edges.py' --paths '$paths' --annotations 'stage/*.bed' --strains '#echo " ".join([str($el.element_identifier) for $el in $annotations])#' --min-overlap $min_overlap --output '$output'

param.label: odgi paths TSV
param.help: The odgi paths output for the PGGB graph. The first column holds PanSN path names of the form SAMPLE#1#CONTIG.

param.label: Per-strain gene BED collection
param.help: A 'list' collection with one gene BED per strain. The element identifier is used as the strain name and must match the SAMPLE component of the PanSN path names. Each BED needs at least 4 columns (chrom, start, end, gene_id); chrom must match the CONTIG component.

param.label: Minimum reciprocal CDS overlap
param.help: Reciprocal CDS coverage threshold for a co-membership edge. Path-level co-membership is recorded with overlap 1.000; this threshold is consumed by the downstream union-find filter.

tool.help:
**Phase E graph edges**
Reads an [odgi](https://github.com/pangenome/odgi) `paths` TSV for a [PGGB](https://github.com/pangenome/pggb) graph together with per-strain gene BED annotations and emits gene-pair edges for genes whose strains share a graph path. This is a third evidence source for the Phase E ortholog clustering and is subsequently filtered by the union-find reciprocal-overlap check in `phase_e_consensus`.
-----
**Inputs**
- *odgi paths TSV* - column 1 holds PanSN path names `SAMPLE#1#CONTIG`. The `SAMPLE` component is the strain, the `CONTIG` component the chromosome. - *Per-strain gene BED collection* - a `list` collection with one BED per strain; the element identifier is the strain name and is staged as `{strain}.bed` so the script's filename glob resolves the strain. Each BED needs >= 4 columns (`chrom`, `start`, `end`, `gene_id`), with `chrom` matching the `CONTIG` component of the path names. - *Minimum reciprocal CDS overlap* - threshold (default 0.90) recorded for the downstream filter.
**Output**
- `graph_edges.tsv` - columns `strain_a`, `gene_a`, `strain_b`, `gene_b`, `path_id`, `overlap`. One row per co-membership gene pair (path-level overlap is reported as `1.000`).
-----
**Implementation note**
The strain list passed to the script is built from the collection element identifiers, so it always matches the staged BEDs. A graph path is treated as shared only when at least two of the listed strains traverse it.
Ported from `Pv4-pangenome/v3/pipeline/impl/scripts/phase_e_graph_edges.py`.
